message:
fix(fcp-protocol/session): negotiate_suite uses responder-picks ordering (br-crkft.2)

Flips iteration in `negotiate_suite` from initiator-picks to responder-picks:
the responder's first-preferred suite that the initiator also supports wins.
This is the modern-crypto default (TLS 1.3, Noise, WireGuard) and defends
against downgrade via a malicious or coerced initiator that deliberately
orders its offers worst-first.

Changes:
- session.rs:810 — flip iteration; loud docstring calling out responder-picks
- Rewrite 2 public-API tests (test_negotiate_suite_responder_preference,
  negotiate_suite_prefers_responder_order) that previously asserted
  initiator-picks
- Rewrite 2 golden-vector tests (negotiation_deterministic,
  negotiation_prefers_responder_order) that also pinned initiator-picks
- Mirror the flip in fcp-conformance/interop/session.rs local helper +
  4 tests; keep conformance/reference parity
- (Incidental) add missing `battery_defer_threshold_percent` field to the
  RepairControllerConfig struct literal in vectors/repair.rs — pre-existing
  compile error surfaced while verifying crkft.2; fixed to unblock tests

Benign today: Suite1 and Suite2 are both cryptographically sound, no
weak option to downgrade to. Becomes meaningful the moment a future
suite deprecates one of the existing ones.

Transcript binding (MeshSessionHello::transcript_bytes includes suites)
protects against in-transit rewriting — a DIFFERENT defense addressing
a different threat model.

Tests: 6 lib + 3 integration + 8 conformance + 73 golden-vector all green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
